access: Metro E (Expo) Line runs straight to Downtown LA in about 50 minutes, no transfers.
night: E Line runs until roughly midnight; rideshare is the safer late-night option.
walk: Downtown Santa Monica station sits in a busy, well-lit pedestrian core.
notes: Best for the DTLA–beach corridor; you'll still want rideshare for everywhere else.

access: Metro B (Red) Line connects Hollywood to Downtown LA and Union Station.
night: B Line runs frequently into the evening; quieter and best avoided alone very late.
walk: Hollywood/Highland and Hollywood/Vine stations are surrounded by foot traffic.
notes: Great for reaching Universal City and DTLA without a car.

access: The main interchange — A, B, D, and E lines all meet around 7th St/Metro Center.
night: Trains run reliably until midnight; use rideshare for late returns.
walk: Stick to the Grand Ave and Arts District corridors; some blocks feel empty at night.
notes: 7th St/Metro Center is the best-connected hub for solo travelers.
